clusters.title: Israeli forces in southern Lebanon as ground operation announced
clusters.neutral_headline: Israeli Forces Launch Ground Operation In Lebanon

items.title: Israeli forces in southern Lebanon as ground operation announced
items.standfirst: Israeli forces attacked multiple towns in southern Lebanon after announcing “limited and targeted ground operations."
items.content: Israeli forces attacked multiple towns in southern Lebanon after announcing “limited and targeted ground operations."

items.title: Israeli strikes hit southern Lebanese town
items.standfirst: Smoke billowed across southern Lebanon on Monday as the Israeli army said it conducted ground operations in "new locations", deploying troops to target Hezbollah positions as it intensified its campaign against the Iran-backed militant group.
items.content: Smoke billowed across southern Lebanon on Monday as the Israeli army said it conducted ground operations in "new locations", deploying troops to target Hezbollah positions as it intensified its campaign against the Iran-backed militant group.

items.title: Israel says 'limited' ground operations under way in Lebanon
items.standfirst: Troops are targeting "Hezbollah strongholds" in south Lebanon, where strikes reportedly killed seven people on Monday.
items.content: Troops are targeting "Hezbollah strongholds" in south Lebanon, where strikes reportedly killed seven people on Monday.

ai_summary: Israeli forces attack multiple towns in southern Lebanon
Ground operations target Hezbollah strongholds
Seven people killed in strikes on Monday
Israeli army deploys troops to new locations
Hezbollah positions targeted in intensified campaign

items.title: Bahrain and Saudi Arabia F1 races both scrapped, organisers confirm
items.standfirst: The Formula 1 races in Bahrain and Saudi Arabia have been cancelled in the wake of the ongoing Iran war, organisers have confirmed.
items.content: The Formula 1 races in Bahrain and Saudi Arabia have been cancelled in the wake of the ongoing Iran war, organisers have confirmed.

items.title: Formula 1 cancels races in Bahrain and Saudi Arabia due to Iran war

items.title: Afghan asylum-seeker dies after less than 24 hours in ICE custody

clusters.title: Car park firm NCP falls into administration, putting nearly 700 jobs at risk
clusters.neutral_headline: Car Park Firm NCP Falls Into Administration

items.title: Car park firm NCP falls into administration, putting nearly 700 jobs at risk
items.standfirst: PwC called in as administrators after company runs out of cash, leaving it unable to pay landlords and creditorsNational Car Parks, the UK’s biggest car park operator, has fallen into administration, putting nearly 700 jobs at risk.NCP’s board of directors called in PwC as administrators after it ran out of cash, leaving it unable to pay its landlords and creditors, with significant rent payments due at the end of March. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Car park firm NCP collapses with nearly 700 jobs at risk
items.standfirst: The car park operator says demand for parking has not recovered to pre-Covid levels, as its administrators look to sell the business.
items.content: The car park operator says demand for parking has not recovered to pre-Covid levels, as its administrators look to sell the business.

ai_summary: NCP falls into administration due to cash shortage
Nearly 700 jobs at risk
Company unable to pay landlords and creditors

Demand for parking not recovered to pre Covid levels

clusters.title: French political parties seek alliances before final round of local elections
clusters.neutral_headline: French Political Parties Seek Alliances Before Local Elections

items.title: French political parties seek alliances before final round of local elections
items.standfirst: Candidates look for deals with rivals to boost chances as major seats including Paris, Marseille and Lyon appear tightPolitical parties in France are hastily attempting to negotiate strategic alliances before the final round of local elections this weekend, after a strong showing by the far right and the radical left.This Sunday’s final-round vote for mayors and local councillors in major cities including Marseille, Lyon and Paris is expected to be close. Continue reading...
items.content: Candidates look for deals with rivals to boost chances as major seats including Paris, Marseille and Lyon appear tightPolitical parties in France are hastily attempting to negotiate strategic alliances before the final round of local elections this weekend, after a strong showing by the far right and the radical left.This Sunday’s final-round vote for mayors and local councillors in major cities including Marseille, Lyon and Paris is expected to be close. Continue reading...

items.title: Negotiations in full swing in French cities over mayoral vote
items.standfirst: Following the first of two rounds of municipal elections on March 15, the race for many of France's bigger cities including Paris and Marseille looks set to be tight. Politicians are now moving to make cross-party alliances against they key opponents, after both the far right and left made inroads.
items.content: Following the first of two rounds of municipal elections on March 15, the race for many of France's bigger cities including Paris and Marseille looks set to be tight. Politicians are now moving to make cross-party alliances against they key opponents, after both the far right and left made inroads.

items.title: What to know from the first round of France's municipal elections
items.standfirst: In France's municipal elections the far right National Rally is gaining momentum, and France Unbowed has made a breakthrough. Both parties treated the municipal elections as a test-run for the presidential race and emerged strengthened from the first round. The left held its ground particularly in Paris, Marseille and Lyon. Here are the key takeaways.
items.content: In France's municipal elections the far right National Rally is gaining momentum, and France Unbowed has made a breakthrough. Both parties treated the municipal elections as a test-run for the presidential race and emerged strengthened from the first round. The left held its ground particularly in Paris, Marseille and Lyon. Here are the key takeaways.

ai_summary: Parties negotiate strategic alliances
Far right and radical left make strong showings
Candidates look for deals to boost chances
Major cities including Paris and Marseille at stake
Final round of local elections to be held

clusters.title: Sarkozy trial 'test to French judicial system' when faced with political pressure
clusters.neutral_headline: Nicolas Sarkozy Appeals Conviction In Libyan Funding Case

items.title: Sarkozy trial 'test to French judicial system' when faced with political pressure
items.standfirst: Former French president Nicolas Sarkozy is on trial once again as he appeals his conviction in the Libyan funding case. Although a guilty verdict is unlikely to change what his base thinks of him, it would undeniably taint his legacy. The trial also sends out a clear message to the political elite over corruption and is a robustness test for the country's judicial system, as FRANCE 24's Camille Knight reports.
items.content: Former French president Nicolas Sarkozy is on trial once again as he appeals his conviction in the Libyan funding case. Although a guilty verdict is unlikely to change what his base thinks of him, it would undeniably taint his legacy. The trial also sends out a clear message to the political elite over corruption and is a robustness test for the country's judicial system, as FRANCE 24's Camille Knight reports.

items.title: Sarkozy's high-stakes appeal in Libyan funding case opens in Paris
items.standfirst: Former French President Nicolas Sarkozy is back in court to appeal criminal charges that he sought financing for his 2007 campaign from former Libyan dictator Muammar Gaddafi. It's one of the many cases to follow Sarkozy since he left office.
items.content: Former French President Nicolas Sarkozy is back in court to appeal criminal charges that he sought financing for his 2007 campaign from former Libyan dictator Muammar Gaddafi. It's one of the many cases to follow Sarkozy since he left office.

items.title: Former president Sarkozy appeals conviction in Libyan funding case
items.standfirst: Former French President Nicolas Sarkozy returned to court on March 16 for an appeal hearing in Paris over his conviction linked to the alleged illegal campaign financing of his 2007 presidential campaign by Libya. The case led him to spend 20 days in prison in 2025. FRANCE 24's Camille Knight tells us what's at stake from the court in Paris.
items.content: Former French President Nicolas Sarkozy returned to court on March 16 for an appeal hearing in Paris over his conviction linked to the alleged illegal campaign financing of his 2007 presidential campaign by Libya. The case led him to spend 20 days in prison in 2025. FRANCE 24's Camille Knight tells us what's at stake from the court in Paris.

items.title: Nicolas Sarkozy back in court for fresh trial over alleged Libya funding
items.standfirst: Ex-French president, who was jailed last year for criminal conspiracy, to be tried at Paris appeal court on four counts Nicolas Sarkozy appeared at the Paris court of appeal to face a fresh trial over allegations he conspired to receive illegal election campaign funding from the regime of the late Libyan dictator Muammar Gaddafi.The former rightwing French president, who was in office between 2007 and 2012, denies any wrongdoing. Continue reading...
items.content: Ex-French president, who was jailed last year for criminal conspiracy, to be tried at Paris appeal court on four counts Nicolas Sarkozy appeared at the Paris court of appeal to face a fresh trial over allegations he conspired to receive illegal election campaign funding from the regime of the late Libyan dictator Muammar Gaddafi.The former rightwing French president, who was in office between 2007 and 2012, denies any wrongdoing. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Sarkozy appeals conviction in Paris court
Alleged illegal campaign financing from Libya
Former president denies any wrongdoing
Case linked to 2007 presidential campaign
Sarkozy spent 20 days in prison in 2025
